checklists.title: Calculate an all-in homeownership budget
steps: Record reliable monthly take-home income and existing obligations.
Choose a conservative maximum monthly housing amount.
Estimate principal and interest under multiple rate scenarios.
Add property taxes, homeowners insurance, mortgage insurance, HOA dues, special assessments, utilities, and routine maintenance.
Add an allowance for major repairs and replacements.
Stress-test the budget for income interruption, insurance increases, tax changes, and urgent repairs.
Set a hard purchase-price and cash-to-close ceiling.

checklists.title: Prepare a mortgage application document packet
steps: Gather government-issued identification and residency documentation requested by the lender.
Gather recent pay statements and employment history.
Gather recent bank, brokerage, retirement, and other asset statements.
Gather tax returns, W-2s, 1099s, or business records applicable to the borrower.
Document debts, recurring obligations, rental or property income, and owned real estate.
Prepare explanations and evidence for large deposits, gifts, transfers, or unusual credit events.
Store documents in a secure, access-controlled folder and track lender submissions.

checklists.title: Compare U.S. Loan Estimates
steps: Request Loan Estimates for the same loan amount, product, lock period, and assumptions when practical.
Confirm borrower name, property, loan type, term, purpose, and amount.
Compare interest rate, APR, monthly principal and interest, and projected payment changes.
Compare points, lender credits, origination charges, services, taxes, insurance, and prepaid items.
Compare cash to close and verify down-payment assumptions.
Identify whether the rate is locked and record expiration and extension costs.
Ask each lender to explain material differences or unexpected fees.
Record the selected offer and why it best fits affordability and risk preferences.

checklists.title: Conduct an independent home inspection
steps: Check inspector qualifications, scope, sample report, conflicts, references, and insurance.
Confirm the inspection covers visible structure, roof, exterior, interior, electrical, plumbing, HVAC, drainage, and safety conditions.
Schedule early enough to permit specialist follow-up within contract deadlines.
Attend the inspection when possible and ask how systems operate.
Request photographs, severity, safety impact, likely cause, and recommended specialist for each material issue.
Separate urgent safety defects, major capital items, maintenance items, and cosmetic issues.
Obtain repair estimates where decisions depend on cost.
Track negotiations, credits, repairs, contingency decisions, and final verification.

checklists.title: Review an HOA or common-interest-development package
steps: Read declaration, CC&Rs, bylaws, rules, architectural guidelines, and enforcement policies.
Review current dues, recent increases, delinquency levels, collection policy, and pending special assessments.
Review budget, reserve study, reserve funding, insurance, financial statements, and recent audits.
Review board and member meeting minutes for recurring defects, disputes, projects, and financial concerns.
Check litigation, construction-defect claims, insurance claims, and lender eligibility concerns.
Verify rental, pet, parking, vehicle, solar, exterior-change, and home-business restrictions.
Confirm what the HOA and owner respectively maintain and insure.
Escalate unresolved legal, financial, or insurance concerns to qualified professionals before document contingencies expire.

checklists.title: Verify real-estate wire instructions safely
steps: Treat emailed or changed wire instructions as unverified.
Use a previously verified phone number or in-person contact for the escrow or settlement holder.
Read back the receiving bank, account name, routing number, account number, and exact amount.
Confirm whether any last-minute change is legitimate using a second trusted channel.
Send a small test transfer first when permitted and useful.
Confirm receipt directly with the settlement holder.
Immediately contact the financial institution and law enforcement if fraud is suspected.

checklists.title: Review a U.S. Closing Disclosure
steps: Compare loan amount, product, interest rate, term, and monthly payment with the selected Loan Estimate.
Confirm whether the rate is fixed or adjustable and review any prepayment penalty or balloon payment.
Reconcile closing costs, lender credits, seller credits, deposits, prorations, and cash to close.
Verify taxes, insurance, escrow setup, HOA charges, and prepaid items.
Confirm names, property address, vesting information, and settlement contacts are accurate.
Ask for explanations and corrected documents for discrepancies before signing.
Verify final payment and wire instructions through a trusted channel.

checklists.title: Review California natural-hazard disclosures
steps: Confirm the Natural Hazard Disclosure identifies the correct parcel.
Review whether the parcel lies in mapped flood, dam-inundation, very-high fire, wildland fire, earthquake-fault, or seismic-hazard areas listed by the disclosure.
Do not treat the disclosure as a complete property-risk assessment; review current official maps and reports.
Ask insurers about availability, exclusions, deductibles, mitigation requirements, and realistic premiums before removing applicable contingencies.
Evaluate evacuation routes, drainage, slope, vegetation, soil, and adjacent hazards with relevant specialists when indicated.
Document how each material hazard changes price, insurance, maintenance, retrofit, emergency, and exit plans.

checklists.title: Estimate California property taxes and special assessments
steps: Identify the parcel and current secured property-tax bill.
Separate the base ad valorem tax from voter-approved debt and direct assessments.
Identify Mello-Roos, community-facilities-district, landscape, lighting, school, or other parcel assessments.
Estimate taxes using the expected purchase price rather than the seller's current assessed value.
Estimate supplemental taxes caused by reassessment after purchase.
Confirm whether the lender's escrow estimate includes all recurring items and whether supplemental bills remain the owner's responsibility.
Keep a cash reserve for bills arriving after closing.

checklists.title: Review a Contra Costa County property-tax bill
steps: Match the assessor parcel number and property address.
Review land, improvement, and total assessed values.
Review the tax-rate area and all fixed or direct assessments.
Check installment due dates, delinquency dates, and payment status.
Identify whether the displayed bill is informational because a lender is expected to pay it.
Confirm how prorations appear in escrow documents.
Save the bill and payment evidence with closing records.

checklists.title: Estimate and prepare for Contra Costa supplemental property tax
steps: Use expected purchase value and the parcel's prior assessed value to estimate the supplemental assessment difference.
Account for the purchase date because it determines the months covered and whether more than one supplemental bill may result.
Use the county estimator or request clarification from the Assessor or Tax Collector.
Reserve cash separately from the ordinary annual tax and lender impound account.
Monitor mail and county records after closing because failure to receive a bill does not remove payment responsibility.
Review the assessed value and appeal process promptly if the value appears incorrect.

checklists.title: Apply for the Contra Costa homeowner's exemption
steps: Confirm the home is the applicant's eligible principal residence.
Obtain the current claim form and instructions from the Contra Costa County Assessor.
Verify occupancy-date and filing-deadline requirements for the annual and any supplemental assessment.
Submit the claim through the approved channel and retain confirmation.
Check later assessment or tax records to confirm the exemption was applied.
Contact the Assessor promptly if the exemption is missing or facts change.

checklists.title: Review San Ramon permit history
steps: Confirm the parcel and address are within the City's permitting jurisdiction.
Request or search available building, electrical, mechanical, plumbing, planning, solar, pool, and code-enforcement records.
Compare permits with the current layout, listing, disclosures, inspection observations, and assessor data.
Check whether permits were issued, inspected, finalized, expired, withdrawn, or left open.
Investigate additions, garage conversions, bedroom or bathroom changes, decks, retaining walls, HVAC, panels, plumbing, pools, and solar systems.
Ask the City what correction, legalization, demolition, or new-permit process may apply; do not assume unpermitted work can be approved.
Estimate cost, timing, insurance, appraisal, financing, safety, and resale impact before contingency deadlines.

checklists.title: Verify San Ramon zoning and land-use context
steps: Confirm city limits, parcel, zoning district, and applicable specific plan.
Review permitted use, setbacks, height, parking, lot coverage, accessory dwelling unit, and other relevant standards.
Identify overlays, design guidelines, easements, HOA restrictions, and Dougherty Valley-specific requirements when applicable.
Review nearby zoning and planned projects that could affect traffic, views, noise, services, or property use.
Describe intended additions, rental use, home business, or site changes to Planning staff and document the response.
Treat feasibility as unresolved until applicable planning, building, fire, utility, HOA, and title constraints are checked.

checklists.title: Identify San Ramon utility providers for an address
steps: Confirm the exact service address and parcel.
Identify the applicable water provider rather than assuming one provider serves all addresses.
Identify the applicable wastewater or sewer provider.
Confirm garbage, electricity, gas, internet, and any HOA-managed services.
Ask about account-transfer timing, deposits, inspections, meter access, unpaid-balance handling, and start dates.
Record provider contacts and schedule service activation around possession.

checklists.title: Perform a final home walk-through
steps: Bring the contract, repair agreement, inspection summary, disclosure updates, and included-items list.
Confirm the property is in the expected condition and agreed repairs appear complete.
Test accessible lights, outlets, appliances, plumbing fixtures, drains, HVAC, doors, windows, locks, garage equipment, and safety devices.
Check for new leaks, damage, removed fixtures, debris, pests, or occupancy issues.
Verify keys, remotes, codes, manuals, warranties, permits, and seller-provided records.
Photograph meter readings and material concerns.
Notify the agent and settlement team before closing when contractual resolution is needed.
